name: Fryk 2023 MAKROTAD Type 2 Diabetes Microcirculation Extract
population_or_context: Men without apparent erectile dysfunction age 40-70 and postmenopausal women age 55-70 with well-controlled type 2 diabetes, BMI 27-40 kg/m2.
sample_size: 23 enrolled; 18 included in full analysis set.
methods: Single-center double-blind randomized placebo-controlled two-period crossover phase 2 trial of tadalafil 20 mg daily and placebo for 6 weeks each, separated by 8-week washout; primary endpoint was whole-body insulin resistance, with microcirculation, endothelial function, HbA1c, metabolic markers, and tolerability as secondary or phenotyping outcomes.
population_profile: Adults with well-controlled type 2 diabetes and overweight/obesity; partially overlaps men over 40 but not mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 20 mg once daily for 6 weeks in crossover design.
indication_context: Experimental metabolic-vascular trial in type 2 diabetes.
study_design: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled crossover phase 2 trial.
safety_findings: Muscle pain, dyspepsia, and headache were more frequent with high-dose tadalafil; no serious-adverse-event difference appeared between treatments in the abstract.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate: direct tadalafil exposure but high-dose daily use in type 2 diabetes rather than healthy 30-50 users.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small feasibility trial, secondary endpoints, crossover design, selected well-controlled T2D population, and no cardiovascular clinical outcomes.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence; transfer limit is high-dose T2D metabolic-vascular context and secondary endpoint status.
